question: What is credit?
options: An agreement to receive money now and pay it back later
Money you find on the street
explanation: Credit is an agreement where a lender gives you money now, and you promise to pay it back later — usually with extra money called interest. It's not free money!

question: What is the range of credit scores?
explanation: Credit scores range from 300 (very poor) to 850 (excellent). Think of it like a grade — the higher, the better! Most people start around 650.

question: Which factor has the BIGGEST impact on your credit score?
options: How many credit cards you have
How much money is in your savings account
explanation: Payment history makes up 35% of your credit score — the biggest chunk! Paying your bills on time, every time, is the #1 thing you can do for a good credit score.

question: Sarah has a credit card with a $1,000 limit. What's the MOST she should owe at any time to keep a healthy credit score?
explanation: Experts recommend keeping your credit utilization below 30%. With a $1,000 limit, that means owing no more than $300. This shows lenders you're not desperate for money!

question: Jake borrowed $500 at 12% annual interest. How much interest will he owe after ONE full year?
explanation: Interest = amount borrowed x interest rate. So $500 x 0.12 = $60. Jake will owe $560 total after one year. The $60 is the cost of borrowing that money!

question: Which type of credit lets you borrow, pay back, and borrow again up to a limit?
explanation: Credit cards are 'revolving credit' — you can use them, pay them off, and use them again up to your limit. Loans give you money once and you pay it back over time.

question: What is a mortgage?
options: A loan specifically for buying a house
explanation: A mortgage is a special long-term loan used to buy a house. It usually takes 15 to 30 years to pay back because houses are really expensive!

question: Which action would HURT your credit score?
options: Paying your bills on time every month
Keeping your credit card balance low
explanation: Missing a payment is one of the worst things for your credit score! Payment history is the #1 factor (35% of your score). Always pay at least the minimum on time.

question: Why is it bad to only make minimum payments on a credit card?
options: The bank will close your account
Interest keeps adding up, so you pay much more over time
Your credit score drops to zero
explanation: Making only minimum payments means interest piles up month after month. A $1,000 balance at 20% interest with minimum payments could take 8+ years to pay off and cost you over $800 in interest!

question: Emma has a credit score of 750. What range does this fall in?
explanation: A score of 750 falls in the 'Very Good' range (740-799). Emma would qualify for great interest rates on loans. Nice job, Emma!

question: Why might a landlord check your credit score before renting you an apartment?
options: They want to know your favorite color
It's required by law to check credit for all renters
A good credit score shows you're likely to pay rent on time
They want to sell you a credit card
explanation: Landlords check credit because a good score shows a history of paying bills on time. It tells them you're likely to pay rent reliably. Credit scores affect more than just borrowing!

question: Two people want to buy the same $20,000 car with a loan. Alex has a 750 credit score and gets 4% interest. Jordan has a 580 credit score and gets 12% interest. Over 5 years, who pays MORE for the same car?
options: Alex, because a higher credit score means higher costs
They pay the same amount since it's the same car
Jordan, because a lower credit score means a higher interest rate
Neither — the dealership sets one price for everyone
explanation: Jordan pays WAY more! At 12% interest, Jordan would pay about $6,700 in interest, while Alex at 4% pays only about $2,100. That's $4,600 MORE for the exact same car. Good credit saves you real money!
